feature: Amazon RDS PostgreSQL Minor Version Upgrade Feature
summary: Successfully tested the RDS PostgreSQL minor version upgrade feature. All backend API endpoints work correctly with LIVE AWS RDS data. Frontend page renders properly showing instance card, upgrade targets (17.5-17.9), and confirmation modal. Navigation link visible in Admin dropdown.

backend_api_tests.details: Returns connected:true, instances array with bigmann-profiles-db, engine_version:17.4, status:available
Returns current_version:17.4, targets array with 5 valid minor versions (17.5-17.9) all with is_valid_target:true
Returns error for non-existent instance as expected
Returns 422 for invalid input data (validation working)

frontend_tests.details: RDS Upgrade link visible in Admin dropdown menu
Page title 'Amazon RDS PostgreSQL Upgrade' renders correctly
bigmann-profiles-db card shows PostgreSQL 17.4, Available status, all instance details (class, storage, encryption, etc.)
Shows 5 upgrade targets: 17.9 (Latest), 17.8, 17.7, 17.6, 17.5 - all with 'available' badges
Clicking target enables upgrade button, button shows 'Upgrade to PostgreSQL 17.9'
Modal shows instance, current version (17.4), target (17.9), timing, warning message, Cancel and Confirm buttons

seed_data_creation: None - using live AWS RDS data
context_for_next_testing_agent: RDS upgrade feature fully implemented and tested. Backend connects to LIVE AWS RDS and returns real data. The bigmann-profiles-db instance is running PostgreSQL 17.4 and can be upgraded to 17.5-17.9. POST /api/cve/iac/rds/upgrade endpoint exists but was NOT tested with actual upgrade to avoid modifying production database. Frontend page at /admin/rds-upgrade requires admin login and displays all components correctly.
important_notes: All APIs connect to LIVE AWS RDS - NO MOCKED DATA
POST upgrade endpoint NOT tested with real data to avoid modifying production database
Instance currently on PostgreSQL 17.4, upgrades available to 17.5-17.9
